commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=f37029e6756fd68d474042bde8cd0437d7773ddf)
commands.review_summary:
The PR wires the existing Skill Workshop view/controller into the Control UI app shell, adds app-level workshop state, scopes Previous/Next to filtered proposals, and removes the redundant queue-width commit callback.

PR surface: Source +114. Total +114 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main maps /skills/workshop and loads proposals, but renderApp has no skillWorkshop branch. The remaining PR bug is also source-reproducible because filter/search handlers only mutate state while the view falls back to filtered[0] without loading it.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor or maintainer action is needed to fix the filtered-selection/detail-loading bug and add real behavior proof; ClawSweeper repair is not selected because the external PR still needs contributor-visible proof after code changes.

target.title: fix(ui): render skill workshop tab
target.body:
## Summary
- Render the Skill Workshop tab through the Control UI app shell.
- Wire the existing Skill Workshop lifecycle actions, revision handoff, file preview, mode, filters, and layout classes into `renderApp`.
- Keep Board Previous/Next navigation scoped to the currently visible filtered proposal list.
- Reuse the Skill Workshop controller state contract from `AppViewState` to avoid duplicating the workshop field list.

## Bug / Behavior
The route and navigation entry for Skill Workshop already existed, and `app-settings.ts` already attempted to load proposals for `skillWorkshop`, but `renderApp` never rendered the lazy Skill Workshop view. Opening `/skills/workshop` selected the tab without any workshop body.

After wiring the view, a secondary navigation bug was visible: the view filters proposals before displaying a selected item, while the parent Previous/Next handler was navigating the full hidden proposal list. If the selected key was filtered out, for example after applying or rejecting a pending proposal or changing status/search filters, the detail pane could show the first visible proposal while Previous/Next skipped through hidden proposals or appeared stuck.

## Root Cause
`renderApp` had no `skillWorkshop` render branch or Skill Workshop content layout classes. Once that branch existed, relative Board navigation also needed to use the same visible proposal set as `renderSkillWorkshop`, not the raw `state.skillWorkshopProposals` array.

## Fix
- Add a lazy Skill Workshop render branch in `ui/src/ui/app-render.ts`.
- Apply `content--skill-workshop` and `content--skill-workshop-today` classes for the existing workshop CSS.
- Pass Skill Workshop state and lifecycle callbacks to the existing view/controller.
- Use `filterSkillWorkshopProposals(...)` for relative proposal navigation and select index `0` when no visible proposal is currently selected.
- Remove the redundant queue width commit callback and use a single width-change handler.
- Extend/reuse `SkillWorkshopState` for app view state typing.

## Verification
- `pnpm format:check -- ui/src/ui/app-render.ts ui/src/ui/views/skill-workshop.ts ui/src/ui/app-view-state.ts ui/src/ui/app.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/skill-workshop.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:ui`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Opening `/skills/workshop` now reaches the `skillWorkshop` route and renders the Skill Workshop surface through the Control UI shell. Board relative navigation now uses the currently visible filtered proposal list, so hidden applied/rejected/search-filtered proposals no longer drive Previous/Next.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw Control UI on macOS with Playwright Chromium. I tested the live Vite route at `http://127.0.0.1:5174/skills/workshop`, then used the repo's Control UI E2E mock Gateway helper so the app could connect and render the workshop body.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started `pnpm --dir ui dev --host 127.0.0.1 --port 5174`, opened `/skills/workshop` with Playwright Chromium, then ran a Playwright Chromium pass against `startControlUiE2eServer()` plus `installMockGateway()` with `skills.proposals.list` returning an empty manifest. I also ran the verification commands listed above.

Evidence after fix: The live Vite route reported `control-ui.tab.visible {previousTab: chat, tab: skillWorkshop, ...}` and page state `location: "/skills/workshop"`, `appTab: "skillWorkshop"`. With the mock Gateway connected, Playwright reported `connected: true`, `workshopPresent: true`, `contentClass: "content    content--skill-workshop content--skill-workshop-today"`, `textIncludesNoProposals: true`, and Gateway requests including `skills.proposals.list`. Screenshot captured at `.artifacts/pr-89135/skill-workshop-rendered-mock-gateway.png`.

Observed result after fix: The Control UI selected `skillWorkshop` for `/skills/workshop`; with a connected mock Gateway, the Skill Workshop DOM rendered and displayed the real view's `No proposals yet` empty state.

What was not tested: I did not run the full UI suite or validate against a live production Gateway containing real skill proposals.
